name_en: Documents of the Mitani family
description_en: A collection of documents, classified as a tangible cultural property (historical material). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2007. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Bust of Mitani Chōzaburō
description_en: A bust statue, classified as a tangible cultural property (historical material). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2002. It is located in Miyamoto Park at Sotokanda 2-19, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Edojō Honmaru, Nishinomaru Goten Kenchiku Zumen
name_en: Architectural drawings of the Honmaru and Nishinomaru palaces of Edo Castle
description_en: A set of architectural drawings, classified as a tangible cultural property (historical material). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2022. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Mizuno Katsukuni's photograph album of the Great Kantō Earthquake
description_en: A photograph album, classified as a tangible cultural property (historical material). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on May 30, 2025. It is located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Stone ox
description_en: A stone ox figure, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2010. It is located in the precincts of Hirakawa Tenmangū Shrine at Hirakawachō 1-7-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Pair of stone lions (male and female)
description_en: A pair of stone lion figures, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1991. They are located in the precincts of Kanda Shrine at Sotokanda 2-16-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Iron rainwater basin of Ōdenmachō Yagumo Shrine
description_en: An iron rainwater basin, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2004. It is located in the precincts of Kanda Shrine at Sotokanda 2-16-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Group of stone objects related to the Kagoso confraternity
description_en: A group of stone objects, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2005. They are located in the precincts of Kanda Shrine at Sotokanda 2-16-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Household utensils of merchant families in Kanda
description_en: A collection of household utensils, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1997. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Iron rainwater basin of Kobunachō Yagumo Shrine
description_en: An iron rainwater basin, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2005. It is located in the precincts of Kanda Shrine at Sotokanda 2-16-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Guardian dog statues (Tsukudo Shrine)
description_en: Stone guardian dog statues (komainu), classified as a tangible folk cultural property. They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1996. They are located in the precincts of Tsukudo Shrine at Kudankita 1-14-21, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Guardian dog statues (Hie Shrine)
description_en: Stone guardian dog statues (komainu), classified as a tangible folk cultural property. They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2000. They are located in the precincts of Hie Shrine at Nagatachō 2-10-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Guardian dog statues (Hirakawa Tenmangū Shrine)
description_en: Stone guardian dog statues (komainu), classified as a tangible folk cultural property. They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1995. They are located in the precincts of Hirakawa Tenmangū Shrine at Hirakawachō 1-7-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Perpetual votive lantern
description_en: A votive lantern, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2005. It is located in the precincts of Hirakawa Tenmangū Shrine at Hirakawachō 1-7-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Water basin (Kanda Shrine)
description_en: A stone water basin, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2002. It is located in the precincts of the Konpira and Mishuku Inari subsidiary shrines within Kanda Shrine at Sotokanda 2-16-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Water basin (Shinpō-ji Temple)
description_en: A stone water basin, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2002. It is located in the precincts of Shinpō-ji Temple at Kōjimachi 6-4-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Water basin (Misaki Inari Shrine)
description_en: A stone water basin, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2014. It is located in the precincts of Misaki Inari Shrine at Misakichō 2-9-12, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Festival float figure 'Kumasaka'
description_en: A festival float figure, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1988. It is located at Kanda Shrine, Sotokanda 2-16-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Festival float figure of a drum beater, commonly called 'Teketen Kozō'
description_en: A festival float figure, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2007. It is located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Head of the festival float figure 'Hida no Takumi'
description_en: The head of a festival float figure, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1997. It is located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Strength-testing stones (Kanda Shrine)
description_en: Strength-testing stones (chikaraishi), classified as a tangible folk cultural property. They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1991. They are located in the precincts of Kanda Shrine at Sotokanda 2-16-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Strength-testing stones (Marshal Tōgō Memorial Park)
description_en: Strength-testing stones (chikaraishi), classified as a tangible folk cultural property. They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1992. They are located in Marshal Tōgō Memorial Park at Sanbanchō 18, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Strength-testing stones (Tsukudo Shrine)
description_en: Strength-testing stones (chikaraishi), classified as a tangible folk cultural property. They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1989. They are located in the precincts of Tsukudo Shrine at Kudankita 1-14-21, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Strength-testing stone (the Tenryūseki, Hirakawa Tenmangū Shrine)
description_en: A strength-testing stone (chikaraishi), classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1993. It is located in the precincts of Hirakawa Tenmangū Shrine at Hirakawachō 1-7-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Group of strength-testing stones
description_en: A group of strength-testing stones (chikaraishi), classified as a tangible folk cultural property. They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1989. They are located in the precincts of Yanagimori Shrine at Kanda Sudachō 2-25-1, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Iron rainwater basin
description_en: An iron rainwater basin, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1999. It is located in the precincts of Kanda Shrine at Sotokanda 2-16-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Hundred-prayers stone (hyakudoishi)
description_en: A stone marker used for the hundred-prayers devotion, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2003. It is located in the precincts of Hirakawa Tenmangū Shrine at Hirakawachō 1-7-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Group of stone monuments related to the Fuji confraternity
description_en: A group of stone monuments, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1998. They are located in the precincts of Yanagimori Shrine at Kanda Sudachō 2-25, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Fujikō Saishigu tsuketari Tohai Shōzoku Isshiki
name_en: Ritual implements of the Fuji confraternity, with a full set of pilgrimage garments
description_en: A set of ritual implements with accompanying pilgrimage garments, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on July 1, 1986. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Brush mound (fudezuka)
description_en: A memorial mound for used writing brushes, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2015. It is located in the precincts of Hirakawa Tenmangū Shrine at Hirakawachō 1-7-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Ehon Chakushoku Genji Monogatari Akashi, Miotsukushi Zu Hanabusa Itchō Hitsu Rokkyoku Byōbu
name_en: Akashi and Miotsukushi chapters of the Tale of Genji by Hanabusa Itchō, color on paper, six-panel folding screen
description_en: A painted six-panel folding screen attributed by inscription to Hanabusa Itchō, classified as a tangible cultural property (painting and calligraphy). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on July 1, 1986. It is located at Hie Shrine, Nagatachō 2-10-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Kenpon Chakushoku Kōbai Zu Tokugawa Tsunayoshi Hitsu
name_en: Red plum blossoms by Tokugawa Tsunayoshi, color on silk
description_en: A painting attributed by inscription to Tokugawa Tsunayoshi, classified as a tangible cultural property (painting and calligraphy). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1997. It is located at Hie Shrine, Nagatachō 2-10-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Kenpon Chakushoku Sannō Shatō no Zu
name_en: View of the Sannō shrine precincts, color on silk
description_en: A painting, classified as a tangible cultural property (painting and calligraphy). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2004. It is located at Hie Shrine, Nagatachō 2-10-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Kenpon Bokuga Iwa ni Sekirei Zu Tokugawa Tsunayoshi Hitsu
name_en: Wagtail on a rock by Tokugawa Tsunayoshi, ink on silk
description_en: An ink painting attributed by inscription to Tokugawa Tsunayoshi, classified as a tangible cultural property (painting and calligraphy). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1997. It is located at Hie Shrine, Nagatachō 2-10-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Shihon Chakushoku Kanda Myōjin Sairei Emaki Sumiyoshi Hirotsura Hitsu
name_en: Illustrated handscroll of the Kanda Myōjin festival by Sumiyoshi Hirotsura, color on paper
description_en: A painted handscroll attributed by inscription to Sumiyoshi Hirotsura, classified as a tangible cultural property (painting and calligraphy). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1994. It is located at Kanda Shrine, Sotokanda 2-16-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Shihon Chakushoku Kanda Myōjin Sairei Zukan
name_en: Illustrated handscroll of the Kanda Myōjin festival, color on paper
description_en: A painted handscroll, classified as a tangible cultural property (painting and calligraphy). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2013. It is located at Kanda Shrine, Sotokanda 2-16-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Shihon Chakushoku Butsu Nehan Zu Hakukyō Hitsu
name_en: Nirvana of the Buddha by Hakukyō, color on paper
description_en: A Buddhist painting attributed by inscription to Hakukyō, classified as a tangible cultural property (painting and calligraphy). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2000. It is located at Shinpō-ji Temple, Kōjimachi 6-4-2, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Shihon Bokuga Tansai Kareno Kiji Zu Tokugawa Ieharu Hitsu tsuketari Hairyō Ohitsu no Oe Soegaki
name_en: Pheasant in a withered field by Tokugawa Ieharu, ink and light color on paper, with an accompanying note on the bestowed painting
description_en: A painting attributed by inscription to Tokugawa Ieharu, with an accompanying document, classified as a tangible cultural property (painting and calligraphy). It was designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 1998. It is located at Hie Shrine, Nagatachō 2-10-5, Chiyoda Ward.

name_en: Art materials of the Mitani family
description_en: A collection of art materials, classified as a tangible cultural property (painting and calligraphy). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2008. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.

name_romaji: Sugiyama Keiji Sho 'Dai Nippon Teikoku Kenpō Happu Hōshukubun' oyobi Kawanabe Kyōsai Hitsu 'Bugaku Ranryōō Zu Makue'
name_en: Calligraphy by Sugiyama Keiji celebrating the promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan, and a curtain painting of the bugaku dance Ranryōō by Kawanabe Kyōsai
description_en: A calligraphic work and a curtain painting, classified as a tangible cultural property (painting and calligraphy). They were designated by Chiyoda Ward on April 1, 2019. They are located at the Hibiya Library and Museum, Hibiya Kōen 1-4, Chiyoda Ward.
